questions.question: Consider a scenario where a user interacts with a touch-sensitive display. Which of the following best describes the functional duality of this component within a computer system?
questions.option_1: It functions exclusively as an input device, capturing the user's touch gestures and commands.
questions.option_2: It acts solely as an output device, displaying visual feedback to the user's actions.
questions.option_3: It serves as both an input device, detecting touch input, and an output device, displaying information and visual responses.
questions.option_4: Its primary role is data storage, retaining user interactions for later processing.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Printed Circuit Boards (PCBs) is INCORRECT?
questions.option_1: Complex devices may use multilayer PCBs to fit more circuitry.
questions.option_2: PCBs serve only desktop computers and not portable devices.
questions.option_3: PCBs provide a base to support and organize hardware parts.
questions.option_4: PCBs hold and connect electronic components using conductive tracks.

questions.question: Consider a computer system with a multi-core processor. What is the primary advantage of having multiple cores compared to a single-core processor with the same clock speed when executing multiple independent tasks simultaneously?
questions.option_1: Increased capacity for the system's main memory (RAM)
questions.option_2: Improved ability to handle and execute multiple separate processes concurrently
questions.option_3: Enhanced speed for executing a single, heavily threaded application
questions.option_4: Reduced power consumption for the overall system

questions.question: Which of the following devices can function as both input and output devices?
Which of the following accurately explains why a touchscreen can be considered both an input and an output device?
questions.option_1: It allows users to interact with visual data and sends touch signals to the computer.
questions.option_2: It processes user data internally and sends it to memory.
questions.option_3: It stores programs and lets users write directly to memory.
questions.option_4: It only displays images but responds to voice commands.

questions.question: When navigating through options in a dialog box or menu using the keyboard, what is the primary function of the Tab key?
questions.option_1: To move the focus to the next available option or control
questions.option_2: To select the currently highlighted option
questions.option_3: To go back to the previous option or control
questions.option_4: To close the current dialog box or menu

questions.question: In modern motherboards, which component is directly connected to the CPU to facilitate high-speed communication?
Which keyboard shortcut is used to save an MS Excel workbook?
Which of the following methods is the quickest way to open an existing MS Excel spreadsheet?
questions.option_1: Double-click the .xlsx file in File Explorer
questions.option_2: Open MS Excel and click Blank Workbook

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut opens the Print dialog box in MS Excel 365?
If you want to save a workbook in MS Excel 365 so that only viewing is allowed and editing is restricted, which feature should you use?
Which of the following best describes the underlying data structure that forms the basis of an electronic spreadsheet like MS Excel?
questions.option_1: A linear sequence of data entries, similar to a text document
questions.option_2: A hierarchical tree structure organizing data into nodes and branches
questions.option_3: Relational database model with interconnected tables and fields
questions.option_4: A two-dimensional grid composed of rows and columns intersecting to form cells

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut is used to quickly save a workbook in MS-Excel 365?
Which element of the MS Excel interface displays the name of the currently active cell or the selected range of cells?
Which of the following accurately describes the function of the 'Spill Range' feature introduced in newer versions of MS Excel?
questions.option_1: It restricts data entry within a specific range of cells based on predefined criteria.
questions.option_2: It dynamically resizes a formula's output range when the formula returns multiple values.
questions.option_3: It automatically saves the workbook to a cloud location at predefined intervals.
questions.option_4: It allows multiple users to simultaneously edit different ranges within the same worksheet.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a mixed cell reference in MS Excel?
In MS Excel, which feature ensures that only a specified portion of the spreadsheet is printed?
Which of the following actions is essential for preserving changes made to an Excel workbook under a new file name?
questions.option_1: Selecting 'Save As' and choosing a new name or location

questions.question: Which type of cell reference in MS Excel modifies both, the column letter and row number automatically when a formula is copied to another location?
Which of the following scenarios would most effectively necessitate the use of a three-dimensional reference within an MS Excel formula?
questions.option_1: Creating a chart that visualizes data points sourced from several different workbooks
questions.option_2: Applying conditional formatting to a range of cells based on values in another worksheet within the same workbook
questions.option_3: Calculating the average sales figures across multiple non-contiguous worksheets for the same product category
questions.option_4: Summing values from a specific cell across a sequence of identically structured worksheets representing monthly data for a year

questions.question: Which of the following scenarios would most likely result in a '#REF!' error in an MS Excel formula?
questions.option_1: ​​​Entering a formula that exceeds the maximum allowed length in Excel
questions.option_2: Attempting to divide a number by zero within a cell
questions.option_3: Deleting a row or column that contains cells referenced by a formula in another worksheet
questions.option_4: Referring to a cell that contains a text value in a mathematical operation

questions.question: In an MS Excel spreadsheet, what does the cell address B5 represent?
questions.option_2: The value 'B5' stored in a cell

questions.question: In MS Excel 2019, what is the primary purpose of the 'Print Preview' feature?
questions.option_3: To view how the sheet will look when printed

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid way to save a workbook in Microsoft Excel?
How do we customise bullets in MS Word?
